description: Construct an explanation based on evidence for how geoscience processes have changed Earth's surface at varying time and spatial scales.

description: Analyze and interpret data on the distribution of fossils and rocks, continental shapes, and seafloor structures to provide evidence of past plate motions.

description: Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities.

description: Map fixed commitments before scheduling study blocks so a weekly plan begins with the real constraints of the week.

description: Protect one anchor block for the highest-priority subject and add short review blocks that are realistic for the student to keep.

description: Check a weekly study plan for conflicts, overloaded days, and missing materials before treating it as final.
